Assess whether the leave goals were achieved upon return.
Verify that medications were taken as instructed upon return.

definicao: Facilitating a relaxed internal state of expanded awareness in the present moment, focusing attention on a sound, object, visual image, breath, or movement.
atividades: Discuss the patient's previous experience with meditation.
Discuss the patient's desire to learn meditation.
Explain that during meditation, the person remains calm, yet alert.
Provide quiet time free from interruptions.

definicao: Facilitating family presence in supporting individuals undergoing cardiopulmonary resuscitation and/or invasive procedures.
atividades: Introduce yourself to the team treating the patient and family.
Determine the suitability of the physical location for family presence.
Obtain team consensus for family presence and the period they may remain present.
Notify the treatment team of the family's emotional reaction to the patient's condition, as appropriate.
Obtain information regarding the patient's status, response to treatment, and identified needs.
Introduce yourself and other support team members to the family and patient.
Communicate information regarding the patient's current status in a timely manner.
Assure the family that the patient is receiving the best possible care.
Use the patient's name when speaking with the family.
Determine the emotional, physical, psychosocial, and spiritual support needs of the patient and family and initiate measures to meet those needs.
Determine the psychological burden of the prognosis for the family.
Promote realistic hope, as appropriate.
Be a mediator for the family, as appropriate.
Prepare the family to ensure they are informed about what to expect, what they will see, hear, and/or smell.
Inform the family about expectations and limits of behavior.
Provide a dedicated staff person to ensure family members are never left unattended at the bedside.
Accompany the family to the resuscitation or treatment area and announce their presence to the treatment team each time the family enters the treatment area.
Provide information and explanations about interventions, medical/nursing jargon, and expectations about the patient's response to treatment.
Accompany the family out of the room when requested by the team providing direct patient care.
Provide the family with the opportunity to ask questions and see, touch, and speak with the patient before transfers.
Assist the patient or family members in making phone calls, if needed.
Offer and provide comfort and support measures, including appropriate referrals, as needed.
Participate in assessing the emotional needs of the team and oneself.
Assist in identifying the need for critical incident stress planning, individual stress reduction, etc., as appropriate.
Participate in, initiate, and/or coordinate family grief follow-up at regular intervals, as appropriate.

definicao: Promoting beneficial visits from family and friends.
atividades: Determine the patient's preferences regarding visiting and information release.
Consider ethical and legal implications regarding the patient, family visiting, and the right to information.
Determine the need to limit visits in the case of excessive numbers of people, restless or tired patient, or due to physical status.
Determine the need for a greater number of family and friend visits.
Identify specific problems with visits, if any.
Establish flexible, patient-centered visiting policies, as appropriate.
Prepare the environment for the visit.
Discuss visiting policies with family/significant other(s).
Discuss the overnight stay policy for family/significant other(s).
Discuss the family's understanding of the patient's condition.
Negotiate with family/significant other(s) responsibilities and activities that assist the patient, such as feeding.
Establish ideal times for visits by family/significant other(s).
Explain the reason for limited visiting time.
Periodically evaluate with the family and patient whether visiting practices are meeting the patient/family needs and review accordingly.
Inform visitors, including children, about what to expect to see and hear before the first hospital visit, when appropriate.
Explain the procedure performed.
Encourage family members to use touch as well as verbal communication, as appropriate.
Place a chair next to the bed.
Be flexible with visiting, maintaining rest periods.

definicao: Fostering the growth of the patient's capacity to identify, connect with, and draw upon the source of meaning, purpose, comfort, strength, and hope in their lives.
atividades: Demonstrate caring presence and comfort by spending time with the patient, as well as with the patient's family and other significant person(s).
Encourage dialogue that helps the patient resolve their spiritual issues.
Model healthy reasoning and relationship skills.
Assist the patient in identifying barriers and attitudes that hinder growth or self-discovery.
Offer prayer support individually or in groups, as appropriate.
Encourage participation in devotional services, retreats, and special prayer/study programs.
Promote relationships with others for friendship and work.
Encourage the use of spiritual rituals and celebrations.
Encourage the patient's examination of their degree of spiritual commitment based on beliefs and values.
Provide an environment that fosters a meditative/contemplative attitude for self-reflection.
Assist the patient in exploring beliefs related to healing of body, mind, and spirit.
Refer to support groups, mutual self-help, or other spiritually based programs, as appropriate.
Refer to pastoral care or primary spiritual caregiver when concerns warrant.
Refer for additional body-mind-spirit connection guidance and support, as needed.

definicao: Assisting an individual's desire to replace feelings of anger and resentment towards another, oneself, or a higher power with charity, empathy, and humility.
atividades: Identify the patient's beliefs that may hinder/help in 'letting go' of a problem.
Acknowledge when anger and resentment are justified.
Identify the source of anger and resentment when possible.
Listen empathetically, without moral judgment or offering platitudes.
Explain that forgiveness is a process.
Assist the patient in exploring feelings of anger, bitterness, and resentment.
Use presence, touch, and empathy to facilitate the process, as appropriate.
Explore possibilities for making amends and reconciling with oneself, others, and/or a higher power.
Assist the patient in examining the health and healing dimensions of forgiveness.
Assist the patient in overcoming blocks to healing using spiritual practices (e.g., prayers, guidance, and discernment; healing, touch, healing visualization, and giving thanks), as appropriate.
Teach the art of emotional release and relaxation.
Assist the patient in seeking an arbiter (objective party) to facilitate the process of individual or group interest.
Encourage the use of traditional faith rituals, as appropriate (e.g., anointing, confession, reconciliation).
Communicate higher power/God or forgiveness to the inner self through prayer, scripture, other readings, as appropriate.
Communicate acceptance of the individual's level of progress.

definicao: Assisting another to cope with painful feelings of real or perceived responsibility.
atividades: Guide the patient/family in identifying painful feelings of guilt.
Assist the patient/family in identifying and analyzing the situations in which these feelings are experienced or generated.
Assist the patient/family in identifying their behaviors in the guilt situation.

definicao: Assisting in the resolution of a significant loss.
atividades: Identify the loss.
Assist the patient in identifying the nature of the attachment to the lost object or person.
Assist the patient in identifying the initial reaction to the loss.
Encourage expression of feelings about the loss.
Listen to expressions of grief.
Encourage discussion of previous loss experiences.
Encourage the patient to verbalize memories of the loss, both past and present.
Make empathic and sensitive statements about grief.
Encourage identification of the greatest fears related to the loss.
Instruct on the stages of the grieving process, as appropriate.
Support progression through personal stages of the grieving process.
Include other significant people in discussions and decisions, as appropriate.
Assist in identifying personal coping strategies.
Encourage the patient to implement cultural, religious, and social customs associated with the loss.
Communicate acceptance of discussing the loss.
Respond to children's questions associated with the loss.
Use clear words such as 'dead' or 'died' instead of euphemisms.
Encourage children to discuss their feelings.
Encourage expression of feelings in a manner comfortable for the child, such as writing, drawing, or playing.
Assist the child in clarifying misconceptions.
Identify community support groups.
Support previous conflict resolution efforts, as appropriate.
Reinforce progress made in the grieving process.
Assist in identifying necessary
